title: storage: store UID/GID maps on container layer when using shifting
body:
when the driver supports shifting, set the rechown target to identity instead of the container's maps.

Closes: https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHEL-160859
Assisted-by: Claude Opus 4.6

comments.body:
> Diff() then uses the stored maps via ToContainer() to properly translate host UIDs back to container UIDs in the generated tar.

… because `layerStore.Diff` always uses the ID maps stored with the layer.

---

Is this a fair summary or the basic design?
- For _image_ layers, we generally only store host-mapped layers on the filesystem (#717 would change that). Image layers can be shared across images, and across containers with different mappings.
- If shifting is not supported, at container creation time, `imageTopLayerForMapping` can create an ID-mapped layer ~ = the full contents of the image. (We never do that for parent layers, unless something creates an unrelated container for that parent layer.)
- Container layers are unique to the one container; we store the mapping in the container metadata.
  - If shifting is supported, the container layer uses host mapping, but we mount with a map.
  - If shifting is not supported, the container layer uses the desired mapping (which matches a parent image layer from `imageTopLayerForMapping`)

It’s _pretty likely_ I'm missing something important.

---

Assuming the above is close enough, yes, it’s a problem for the commit path if `Store.Diff` uses the _layer’s_ mapping and not the _container’s_ when the data was created by writing through the container-mapped mount. [Here I’m almost certainly missing something, because if commit were universally broken, I’d think we should have noticed immediately.]

Conceptually, at this point, I’m not sure whether to think about that problem as:
- a confused caller: under the assumption that layers are generally shared across mappings when shifting, the layer is “correctly” non-mapped, and the caller should be calling some kind of future `ContainerDiff` which obtains the mapping from the container. That looks like it would be consistent with the image layers, “if we are using shifting, we are using host mappings for everything”
- a bug in the `CreateContainer`: under the assumption that container layers are never shared, `CreateContainer` should have created the layer with the container-desired ID maps, and this PR is in the direction of fixing that.

I've got to a much smaller (and less controversial) version.

There is more room for cleanup here as follow-ups
